idea-plugin.name: Token Flow
idea-plugin.description:
Make using your tokens intuitive.
Token Flow helps you find the right variable and keep your styles flawless without ever leaving your IntelliJ code.

Token Flow indexes your whole Design System and makes token usage fluid, fast and error-proof.
Supported formats: SCSS variables, CSS Custom Properties, SCSS Maps, TS/JS preset objects, React Native runtime themes and helpers.

Superpowers for Front-end Developers

  • Instant Search (Library)
    A dedicated side panel to find tokens by name, value or category.
    Includes visual swatches and drag-and-drop support.
  • Smart Swapping (Alt+T)
    Need a slightly darker blue or the next spacing scale?
    Token Flow suggests siblings based on HSL proximity, logical order, or scale steps for callable helpers like spacing().
  • Zero "Magic Numbers"
    The IDE flags hardcoded hex codes, pixels or unitless numbers that match an existing token — one click and your code is clean.
  • Inline Suggestions
    As you type a value, matching tokens appear without stealing focus.
  • Deep Context
    Hover any token to see its resolved value, aliases and per-mode variants (Dark / Light / breakpoints).
  • Full-scale Audit
    An "Analyse" tab grades your project health.
    Identify tech debt, unused tokens and hardcoded clusters in seconds.

Real-world Use Cases

  • Rapid Integration — code a UI component without leaving the current file to check the token naming convention.
  • Theme Debugging — see instantly how a token resolves in Dark vs Light mode via the hover popup.
  • System Migration — refactor legacy CSS/SCSS by detecting every hardcoded value that should have been a token.
  • Consistency Check — keep multi-brand projects aligned with Scopes to isolate tokens per platform or sub-project.

Made by

Robin Lopez — designer & front-end engineer.
robinlopez.fr · LinkedIn · Bluesky · Bento
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.1.9 — Smarter broken-reference detection

  • CSS variables declared outside token sources are no longer flagged as broken: Token Flow now picks up two new families of declaration. Runtime injection from component code — Angular [style.--var] host bindings, React/Vue inline-style object keys, setProperty('--var', …) calls. Contextual CSS overrides — any --name: value declaration in any .css/.scss/.sass/.vue rule body, so the CSS Custom Property API pattern (generic component reads var(--c, fallback), consumers set --c locally) just works.
  • Fallbacks no longer hide missing variables: var(--x, inherit) is still flagged when --x is nowhere to be found — a runtime default is not a license to leave a dangling reference.
  • New setting: Settings → Token Flow → Analyser → "Recognise CSS variables declared outside token sources". On by default; uncheck to revert to the strict pre-0.1.9 behaviour.

0.1.8 — Sharper token suggestions (tier & role aware)

  • External token prefixes (per scope): new External tab in Settings → Token Flow → Scopes for CSS-var prefixes injected at runtime by an external framework : PrimeNG --p-, Ionic --ion-, Angular Material --mat-, Material Web --mdc-, Vuetify --v-, Bootstrap --bs-, Radix --radix-, Quasar --q-, Element Plus --el-, Mantine, Carbon, … References matching a configured prefix are never flagged as broken by the Analyser or the Hardcoded Values panel.
  • Auto-detect from package.json: one-click button in the External tab that scans the project for known framework dependencies and adds their prefixes to the scope.
  • Project-open detection notification: opening a project that depends on a known UI framework but has no prefixes configured surfaces a balloon offering Add to all scopes / Configure… / Don't show again. One-shot — never nags twice.
  • JSON config v2: exports/imports now carry externalPrefixes per scope. v1 files remain importable.
  • Hardcoded Values : token-source files are skipped: opening a file declared in any scope's sourcePaths now shows an explanatory banner and disables the scan. No more flagging of --units-xl: 32px; as hardcoded when the file's purpose is to declare it.
  • Hardcoded Values : SCSS map values are no longer flagged: literals inside a $name: (…) or "key": (…) SCSS map (Style-Dictionary / theme-config pattern) are recognised as token definitions and skipped. Handles nested maps to any depth, even when the file isn't formally declared as a token source — useful for mixed files.
  • Hardcoded Values : search & property filter: new search field filters rows by literal, selector, surrounding CSS property, or suggested token (multi-term, case-insensitive). A filter button opens a popup with every detected CSS property grouped by category (Spacing, Color, Radius…), with counts, and a nested search to narrow the property checklist. The previous toolbar toggle "Hide rows without a token suggestion" moves into this popup.
  • Token tier awareness: when several DS tokens share the same value (--units-xl and --spacing-xl both 32px), the suggestion engine now prefers the semantic token over the primitive one. Primitive prefixes recognised: units-, palette-, base-, primitive-, core-, scale-, raw-. So padding: 32px now suggests var(--spacing-xl) instead of var(--units-xl).
  • Property-to-role matching for colors: background, color, border-color, box-shadow… now derive an expected role (surface / content / stroke / effect) and match it against token-name segments. Tokens carrying the right role get a strong boost; tokens carrying a conflicting role are demoted. So background: #005bff prefers token-actions-high-surface-default over global-default-content-default, and color: #001a70 does the opposite.
  • Multi-criterion ranking: the suggestion score was rewritten as a weighted combination of (category, role, tier, exact-vs-fuzzy, length). Token-name length is now only a tiebreaker — a longer-but-more-semantic name can outrank a shorter primitive one, which was impossible before.
  • Fuzzy color tiebreaking: when no exact color match exists, ties at near-identical RGB distance are broken by the semantic score so the role-appropriate token surfaces first.

0.1.7 — Portable scope configuration, smarter Library & Vue SFCs

  • Vue Single File Components (.vue): tokens declared in <style> and <style lang="scss"> blocks of a Vue SFC are now indexed, listed in the Library, counted in audit coverage, and benefit from hardcoded-value detection / completion / Alt+T / Go-to-declaration. Text-based implementation — no dependency on the Vue plugin — so it works on Community and Ultimate. Scanning is confined to inline style blocks, so <template> and <script> sections stay untouched.
  • Export Config: Settings → Token Flow → Scopes now has an Export… link that writes the current scopes to a JSON file. Share it with the team, commit it next to the project, or keep it as a backup.
  • Import Config: paired Import… link reads a JSON file produced by Export. The plugin asks whether to Replace the current scopes or Merge (existing scopes with the same name are overwritten, new ones are appended). Pending edits are persisted via Apply, so a teammate can preview the import before committing.
  • Versioned format: the JSON file carries a schema version. Older Token Flow builds refuse files produced by a future schema instead of silently dropping fields.
  • Library family / sub-family grouping: inside each big category accordion, the Library can now insert a two-level hierarchy of headers — family (HIGH, LOW, PRIMARY) and sub-family (SURFACE, CONTENT, STROKE, …) — derived from the structure of token names alone. No hard-coded vocabulary; the algorithm adapts to any naming convention. Tokens indent under their header so the alignment reads like a tree. New toolbar toggle (group-by icon) next to the view-mode button; off by default — opt in from the panel.
  • Sticky category header: scroll past a category and its header pins to the top of the Library viewport, with the same chevron behaviour as the in-list row.
  • Justified category bar: layout is now [chevron] TITLE … (count), with the count badge tinted so it can't be mistaken for a resolved token value.
  • Two-way kind / file-chip sync: ticking a token-kind in the Library filter popup now flips every file chip of that kind in the strip below the search bar, and vice-versa — the two filter surfaces no longer drift.
  • Variant tooltip — vertical layout: per-variant tooltips now render one line per variant (label left, swatch / glyph + value right) instead of a wide horizontal table that overflowed once a token had several variants. Multi-theme tokens get discrete theme subheaders.
  • Hardcoded Values — tighter rows & hide-no-match filter: rows nest under their selector header, the literal column is narrower, the suggestion combo is taller (no more clipped descenders), and a new toolbar toggle hides literals without any matching token (on by default — broken references stay visible because they're actionable).
  • Light-theme polish: Library category / family / sub-family palette rebuilt with proper light-vs-dark contrast steps (the previous sub-family pair was inverted), and the dark bands that appeared behind separator headers on light themes are gone — gridContainer, the sticky header and the grid viewport now follow list.background.

0.1.6 — Smarter Library, sharper Analyser

  • Multi-term Library search: "informative content" now finds --token-informative-highlight-content-hover. Search splits on spaces/dashes/underscores, every term must match.
  • Library kind filter: the funnel popup gets a Token kind section (CSS / SCSS / JS-JSON) above Families. The button highlights when any filter is active.
  • Context-aware JS token drop: drag or paste a '{path}' token inside a backtick template literal in TS/JS and it lands as ${dt('path')} (or dt('path') inside an existing ${…}).
  • Stale-analysis banner: after a run, the Analyser watches every file referenced by the report; editing one surfaces a yellow banner offering one-click re-run.
  • Reorderable scopes: up/down arrows in Settings, order reflected immediately in the Analyser scope picker.
  • Excluded from Analysis (per scope): new Excludes tab to carve unrelated sub-modules out of a wide scope root.
  • Tabbed scope detail: Sources / Whitelist / Excludes split into tabs — no more vertical scroll in the Settings dialog.
  • Cleaner Analyser toolbar: redundant Refresh / Re-sync scopes removed; the single action flips to Re-run analysis once a report exists.
  • Alt+T preserves dt(…): picking an alternative on ${dt('token.x.y')} no longer rewrites it into a bare Style-Dictionary alias and breaks the surrounding template literal.

0.1.5 — Broken-reference detection & sharper suggestions

  • Broken Reference Detection: the Analyser lists token references that don't resolve (var(--foo), '{token.x.y}', dt('x.y')) in a dedicated section, and the Hardcoded Values panel flags them inline with a yellow warning. A new Reference integrity axis (20 % weight) joins the global score.
  • Resilient JS/TS reference matching: alt+T, completion and go-to now resolve Style-Dictionary refs like '{token.modeLight.informative.default.high.surface.default}' against canonical tokens — leading export binding (token.), mode segment (modeLight / modeDark) and a single camelCase merge / split per path (default.high ↔ defaultHigh) are all tolerated.
  • CSS var() inside TS template literals: alt+T now recognises var(--name) inside backtick strings in .ts/.tsx; CSS functional notations (var, calc, rgb*, hsl*, gradients, transforms) are no longer mistaken for JS helper calls.
  • Category-scoped suggestions: padding: 6px no longer suggests a 6 px shadow-blur token. The value index is keyed by (category, value) with controlled fallback inside the length family (SPACING, RADIUS, SIZING, TYPOGRAPHY, BORDER).
  • Smarter incoherence judgement: --stroke-default: 1px is no longer flagged when the categorizer has already reconciled the name/value clash (here: BORDER).
  • Ignored library files: new Settings list to exclude vendored design-system packages from the analyser.
  • Scanner refinements: values inside comments and inside var(--x, fallback) fallbacks are no longer reported. Standard CSS named colors are detected. Circular token definitions are no longer flagged as hardcoded.
  • UI polish: scope display in the Hardcoded Values panel, scope info right-aligned in toolbars, expandable long Analyser sections, fixed accordion-gap inflation, "Non-existent tokens" renamed to "Broken references".
  • API hygiene: removed all usages of IntelliJ APIs marked deprecated or scheduled-for-removal so the plugin loads cleanly on the latest IDE builds.

0.1.4 — Visual Grid & Polished UX

  • Design System Dashboard - Grid View: New visual mode representing tokens as beautiful, interactive cards. Includes visual previews for colors, spacing, and radius.
  • Smarter Categorization: Added 6 new categories (EFFECTS, LAYOUT, BORDER, etc.) and refined the engine to flawlessly classify complex semantic tokens.
  • Contextual Scope Awareness: The Dashboard and Analyser now dynamically track and display your currently edited file's scope.

0.1.3 — Gutter swatches & SCSS modifiers

  • Gutter color swatches for SCSS: variables ($name) now display a color preview icon in the gutter, resolving through the project index (consistent with native CSS variables).
  • Robust SCSS/CSS parsing: modifiers like !default, !global, and !important are now stripped during extraction, fixing color previews and alias resolution for flagged tokens.

0.1.2 — React Native support

  • React Native runtime themes: property-access tokens (colors.PRIMARY_500, theme.radius.sm, fontPresets.h1.fontSize) are now detected. Typed export const X: Type = {…} aggregators contribute leaf paths only (aligned with barrel re-exports); bare const X = {…} bags keep their prefix. Cross-object alias resolution included.
  • Callable helpers (spacing, radius, …): linear arrow functions like (v) => UNIT * v are indexed with a ƒ badge. Hardcoded 12 now suggests spacing(1.5). Alt+T on spacing(0.5) opens a scale popup (spacing(0.25), spacing(0.5), spacing(1), spacing(1.5), …).
  • Unitless numerics flagged: fontSize: 34, lineHeight: 24, opacity: 0.5 now inspected against the token index (JS/TS only). Numbers inside a helper call are ignored to avoid noise.
  • Suggest matching tokens as you type: the value-completion popup no longer steals editor focus — typing keeps going into the file.
  • Analyser respects the selected scope: file walk is restricted to the scope's rootPath; only the active scope's own catalogues are excluded. Scope changes in Settings refresh the picker live; a Re-sync action is available for manual cache drops.
  • Architecture: TS/JS parsing reorganised into a strategy package (StyleDictionaryParser, RuntimeObjectParser, RuntimeFunctionParser, registry to dispatch per file). Adding a new stack now means plugging in a single class.

0.1.1 — Polish & bug fixes

  • Hover popup — multi-theme grouping: tokens declared under nested SCSS maps ($themes-config -> "themeOne" -> "light" -> …) now render with theme-grouped column headers instead of a flat default | dark | light | dark sequence.
  • Settings — Scopes tab: long intro hints no longer trigger a horizontal scrollbar when the dialog is narrow; HTML paragraphs wrap properly inside the available width.
  • Marketplace metadata cleanup (vendor contact, simplified description).

0.1.0 — First public release

The first iteration of Token Flow ships everything you need to manage your Design System directly inside the IDE.

  • Multi-format indexer — SCSS, CSS, and TS/JS support with alias resolution.
  • Library Tool Window — Visual search, drag-and-drop, and navigation.
  • Alternatives Popup (Alt+T) — Smart sorting for colors and metrics.
  • Hardcoded Inspection — Automatic detection and quick-fix for literals.
  • Analyse Tab — Deep health report and technical debt tracking.
  • Scopes — Multi-UI project support.

localInspection.displayName: Hardcoded value matches a design token

action.description: Scans the project and lists every detected design token
Shows alternatives of the design token under the caret
Opens the file where the token under the caret is declared
